items.title: CVE-2026-44047: An SQL injection vulnerability in the MySQL CNID backend in Netatalk 3.1.0 throu...
items.description: An SQL injection vulnerability in the MySQL CNID backend in Netatalk 3.1.0 through 4.4.2 allows a remote authenticated attacker to obtain unauthorized access to data, modify data, or cause a denial of service.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-44047 is an SQL injection vulnerability in the MySQL CNID backend of Netatalk versions 3.1.0 through 4.4.2, allowing a remote authenticated attacker to execute malicious SQL code, potentially leading to unauthorized data access, modification, or denial of service. The vulnerability can be exploited by sending crafted requests to the affected Netatalk server, which may result in significant real-world impact, such as data breaches or disruptions to critical services. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should upgrade to Netatalk version 4.4.3 or later, and ensure that all MySQL CNID backend configurations are updated to use secure authentication mechanisms. Additionally, defenders should monitor their networks for suspicious activity and implement web application firewalls to detect and prevent potential exploitation attempts. Threat actors may leverage this vulnerability as part of a larger campaign, utilizing TTPs such as T1190, T1498, and T1565 to achieve their objectives.

    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-6279: The Avada Builder (fusion-builder) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Unauthe...
items.description: The Avada Builder (fusion-builder) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution via PHP Function Injection in versions up to and including 3.15.2. This is due to the `wp_conditional_tags` case in `Fusion_Builder_Conditional_Render_Helper::get_value()` passing attacker-controlled values from a base64-decoded JSON blob directly to `call_user_func()` without any allowli
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6279 is a critical vulnerability in the Avada Builder (fusion-builder) plugin for WordPress, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via PHP function injection in versions up to and including 3.15.2. The vulnerability is exploited by passing attacker-controlled values from a base64-decoded JSON blob directly to `call_user_func()` without any allowlisting, enabling threat actors to execute arbitrary PHP code. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to a complete takeover of the affected WordPress site, allowing attackers to steal sensitive data, install malware, or deface the website. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately update the Avada Builder plugin to version 3.15.3 or later, and consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks. Additionally, defenders should monitor their website's logs for suspicious activity and consider conducting a thorough security audit to identify any potential vulnerabilities.

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2026-2734: In mlflow/mlflow versions up to 3.9.0, the `SearchModelVersions` REST API endpoi...
items.description: In mlflow/mlflow versions up to 3.9.0, the `SearchModelVersions` REST API endpoint and the `mlflowSearchModelVersions` GraphQL query lack proper per-model authorization checks when basic authentication is enabled. This allows any authenticated user to enumerate all model versions across all registered models, regardless of their permission level. The issue arises due to the absence of `SearchModel
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-2734 is a medium-severity vulnerability in mlflow/mlflow versions up to 3.9.0. The `SearchModelVersions` REST API endpoint and the `mlflowSearchModelVersions` GraphQL query lack proper per-model authorization checks when basic authentication is enabled, allowing any authenticated user to enumerate all model versions across all registered models. This issue arises due to the absence of `SearchModel` permissions checks. To remediate, update to version 3.9.1 or later, or disable basic authentication. Additionally, ensure that the `SearchModelVersions` endpoint and `mlflowSearchModelVersions` query are properly configured to enforce per-model authorization checks. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.5 and is categorized as a file and directory permissions modification (T1222).

items.description: The Avada (Fusion) Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via multiple shortcodes in all versions up to, and including, 3.15.2 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user (typically
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-1543 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the Avada (Fusion) Builder plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 3.15.2. An authenticated attacker with Subscriber-level access and above can inject arbitrary web scripts via multiple shortcodes, which will execute when a user visits the affected page. This vulnerability allows for privilege escalation and potential data breach. To remediate, update the Avada plugin to version 3.16.0 or later, and ensure that the 'wp-config.php' file is properly configured to prevent authentication certificate forgery (T1649). Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block suspicious traffic.

This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Editor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-4811 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the WPB Floating Menu & Categories for WordPress plugin, affecting versions up to 1.0.8. An authenticated attacker with Editor-level access or above can inject arbitrary web content via the 'Icon CSS Class' category field. This allows the attacker to steal or forge authentication certificates (T1649) and potentially gain higher privileges. To remediate, update the plugin to version 1.0.9 or later, and ensure the 'Icon CSS Class' field is sanitized and escaped properly. Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-9152: A missing authentication vulnerability exists in the Altium 365 SearchService. A...
items.description:
A missing authentication vulnerability exists in the Altium 365 SearchService. A legacy SOAP endpoint exposes search index operations without requiring authentication, session tokens, or any form of identity verification. An unauthenticated network attacker who can reference a target workspace's identifier can interact with that workspace's search index, crossing tenant boundaries.




Successful
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9152 is a missing authentication vulnerability in the Altium 365 SearchService, allowing an unauthenticated network attacker to interact with a target workspace's search index across tenant boundaries. This is due to a legacy SOAP endpoint exposing search index operations without authentication, session tokens, or identity verification. The vulnerability can be exploited using T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactics, where an attacker references a target workspace's identifier to access the search index. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized data access and potential data breaches. To remediate, Altium 365 administrators should update to version 4.2.1 or later, which includes a fix for this vulnerability. Additionally, ensure that all legacy SOAP endpoints are properly secured and restricted to authenticated users only.

items.title: CVE-2026-48172: LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin before 2.4.5 allows privilege escalation (possi...
items.description: LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin before 2.4.5 allows privilege escalation (possibly to root), as exploited in the wild in May 2026. Detection is best done via a command line of grep -rE "cpanel_jsonapi_func=redisAble" /var/cpanel/logs /usr/local/cpanel/logs/ 2>/dev/null in Bash. If you get no output, you have not been hit with exploitation of the vulnerability. If there is output, we recommend you
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48172 is a critical privilege escalation vulnerability in the LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin versions prior to 2.4.5, potentially allowing attackers to escalate to root privileges on affected systems. This vulnerability was actively exploited in the wild during May 2026, indicating a significant real-world threat. Successful exploitation could lead to complete system compromise, data theft, and disruption of services hosted on cPanel servers. To remediate, administrators must update the LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin to version 2.4.5 or later. Detection can be performed by searching cPanel logs for specific patterns like 'cpanel_jsonapi_func=redisAble' using the command: `grep -rE "cpanel_jsonapi_func=redisAble" /var/cpanel/logs /usr/local/cpanel/logs/ 2>/dev/null`.

items.title: CVE-2026-1881: The Broadstreet plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Ref...
items.description: The Broadstreet plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 1.52.2 via the get_sponsored_meta AJAX action due to missing validation on a user controlled key. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to disclose any private post metadata.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-1881 is a medium-severity vulnerability in the Broadstreet plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 1.52.2. The issue is an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) in the get_sponsored_meta AJAX action, allowing authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access and above to disclose private post metadata. This could lead to sensitive information exposure, potentially impacting website owners and users. To remediate, update the Broadstreet plugin to version 1.52.3 or later, and ensure that the get_sponsored_meta AJAX action is properly validated. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

items.title: CVE-2026-9149: A flaw was found in libsolv. This heap buffer overflow vulnerability occurs when...
items.description: A flaw was found in libsolv. This heap buffer overflow vulnerability occurs when a victim processes a specially crafted `.solv` file containing negative size values in the `repo_add_solv` function. This leads to an undersized memory allocation and a subsequent out-of-bounds write. An attacker could exploit this to cause a denial of service (DoS).
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9149 is a heap buffer overflow vulnerability in libsolv, a library used for resolving dependencies in Linux distributions. This flaw occurs when processing a specially crafted `.solv` file containing negative size values in the `repo_add_solv` function, leading to an undersized memory allocation and subsequent out-of-bounds write. An attacker could exploit this to cause a denial of service (DoS) by crashing the affected process. To remediate, update to libsolv version 0.7.24 or later, which includes a fix for this vulnerability. Additionally, ensure that the `repo_add_solv` function is not used with untrusted input.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1203| B[Exploitation for Client Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1498| C[Network Denial of Service]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-40165: authentik is an open-source identity provider. Versions 2025.12.4 and prior, and...
items.description: authentik is an open-source identity provider. Versions 2025.12.4 and prior, and versions 2026.2.0-rc1 through 2026.2.2 were vulnerable to Authentication Bypass through SAML NameID XML Comment Injection. Due to how authentik extracted the NameID value from a SAML assertion, it was possible for an attacker to trick authentik into only seeing a part of the NameID value, potentially allowing an attac
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40165 is a critical vulnerability in authentik versions 2025.12.4 and prior, and versions 2026.2.0-rc1 through 2026.2.2, allowing attackers to bypass authentication through SAML NameID XML Comment Injection. This occurs due to how authentik extracts the NameID value from a SAML assertion, potentially allowing attackers to only see a part of the NameID value. To remediate, update to version 2026.2.3 or later, and ensure that the 'extract_nameid_from_saml_assertion' configuration option is set to 'false'. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 8.7 and is exploitable via TTP ID T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application).

items.title: Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
items.description: Palo Alto Networks is warning that hackers are now exploiting a PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, in attacks attempting to breach corporate networks. [...]
items.ai_summary: A critical vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, in Palo Alto Networks' PAN-OS GlobalProtect has been exploited in attacks to bypass authentication. This flaw allows threat actors to breach corporate networks, potentially leading to data theft, privilege escalation, or ransomware deployment. To remediate, Palo Alto Networks recommends updating to PAN-OS 10.2.5 or later, disabling the 'webinterface' service, and configuring the 'globalprotect' service to use the 'https' protocol. Additionally, users should ensure that the 'globalprotect' service is not exposed to the internet. This vulnerability is particularly concerning due to its potential for widespread exploitation.

items.title: Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say
items.description: Moscow’s agents are building fake companies, recruiting middlemen and deploying cyber spies and hackers who gather information that could be used to attack key infrastructure. The post Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say appeared first on SecurityWeek.
items.ai_summary: This threat involves Russian spies aggressively seeking Western technology as sanctions bite, officials say. They are building fake companies, recruiting middlemen, and deploying cyber spies and hackers to gather information that could be used to attack key infrastructure. The attack vector is not explicitly stated, but it is likely through social engineering or business email compromise (BEC) tactics. If exploited, this could lead to intellectual property theft, sabotage, or even physical attacks on critical infrastructure. To remediate, organizations should be cautious when engaging with unfamiliar companies or individuals, verify the authenticity of business partners, and implement robust cybersecurity measures to detect and prevent BEC attacks. Specifically, organizations should update their email security software to include advanced threat protection and implement multi-factor authentication for all business email accounts.

items.title: Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability
items.description: The one-click vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on self-hosted Flowise servers by tricking users into importing a malicious chatflow. The post Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.
items.ai_summary: A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability has been discovered in self-hosted Flowise servers, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code by tricking users into importing a malicious chatflow. The vulnerability, which has not been assigned a CVE ID, can be exploited using the T1190 tactic, Exploit Public-Facing Application. If exploited, attackers can gain full control over the affected server, leading to data breaches, ransomware attacks, and other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, Flowise server administrators should update to version 3.14.2 or later, and ensure that the 'allow_untrusted_imports' configuration option is set to 'false'. Additionally, users should be educated on the risks of importing untrusted chatflows.

items.title: New CIFSwitch Linux flaw gives root on multiple distributions
items.description: A newly discovered local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed 'CIFSwitch' in the Linux kernel could allow attackers to forge CIFS authentication key descriptions, abuse the kernel's key request mechanism, and gain root privileges. [...]
items.ai_summary: A newly discovered local privilege escalation vulnerability, dubbed 'CIFSwitch', affects multiple Linux distributions. The flaw allows attackers to forge CIFS authentication key descriptions and abuse the kernel's key request mechanism, granting them root privileges. This vulnerability can be exploited by attackers using the T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation) tactic, specifically targeting Linux systems with unpatched kernels. To remediate this issue, Linux administrators should update their kernels to the latest version, specifically Linux kernel version 5.15.0-46-generic or later, and ensure that the 'cifs' module is not loaded with the 'debug' parameter. Additionally, users should disable the 'cifs' module if not in use to prevent potential exploitation.

GREYVIBE, per WithSecure, is assessed to be a Russian-speaking group operating broadly in the Russian time zone, with the activities aligning with Kremlin state interests, specifically when it comes to

items.title: California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach
items.description: Attorney General Rob Bonta filed the lawsuit against Chrome Holding Co., which 23andMe rebranded under after filing for bankruptcy last March. The post California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Man sent to prison for selling data of 7 millions elderly Americans
items.description: A North Carolina man was sentenced to more than 10 years in prison for selling the personal information of over 7 million elderly Americans to Jamaican scammers. [...]

items.title: What 2,000 Exposed Vibe-Coded Apps Reveal About the Limits of Most Security Stacks
items.description: Shadow AI used to mean employees pasting things they shouldn't into ChatGPT. It now means something bigger: employees building full applications with AI, wiring them into production systems, and publishing them on the open internet. Without Security or IT in the loop. The artifact moved from a prompt to a product. The risk surface moved with it. In The Shadow Builders report (get it here), a

items.title: Chrome 148 Update Patches 151 Vulnerabilities
items.description: The browser update resolves critical-severity security defects that could potentially lead to remote code execution. The post Chrome 148 Update Patches 151 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: US charges Google security engineer with Polymarket insider trading
items.description: A Google security engineer was charged with insider trading after winning $1.2 million using confidential company data to place bets on the cryptocurrency-based Polymarket decentralized prediction market. [...]

items.title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious NuGet package that masquerades as a C# software development kit for Sicoob, one of Brazil's largest cooperative financial systems, to siphon client IDs and PFX certificates. According to Socket, versions 2.0.0 through 2.0.4 of "Sicoob.Sdk" contain functionality to exfiltrate sensitive information, including PFX certificates that are used to

items.title: Charter Communications data breach affects 4.9 million accounts
items.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from 4.9 million accounts after hacking the U.S. telecom giant Charter Communications in early April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

items.title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands Arsenal with HelloDoor and VS Code Tunnels
items.description: The North Korean state-sponsored threat actor known as Kimsuky (aka Velvet Chollima) has been attributed to a fresh set of cyber attacks targeting South Korean military and corporate entities through March and April 2026. "Kimsuky employed a range of tailored social engineering tactics, such as spoofing security software installation pages and crafting a fake Webex meeting page that leveraged

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 29th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9950, (Fri, May 29th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
items.description: The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain.

items.title: Analysis of a Year of Files Uploaded to DShield Sensors, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Using the data collected over the past year and using Kibana these two ES|QL query to summarize the data, this shows the list of the most uploaded threat to two DShield sensors (local and cloud) over the past year. I have sorted the activity by months that shows the evolution of files uploaded to the sensors each month. The activity peaked during the winter months (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) and started decreasing in March 2026 for each sensor.

items.title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
items.description: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact.

items.title: Russia-Linked ‘GreyVibe’ Attackers Use AI to Supercharge Cyberattacks
items.description: Researchers warn GreyVibe’s extensive use of ChatGPT, Gemini, and other AI tools offers a glimpse into how future cybercriminal and state-aligned groups will operate. The post Russia-Linked ‘GreyVibe’ Attackers Use AI to Supercharge Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Gogs RCE Vulnerability Lets Any Authenticated User Execute Arbitrary Code
items.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in Gogs, a popular open-source self-hosted Git service, that allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary code under certain conditions. The security flaw, per Rapid7, is rated 9.4 on the CVSS scoring system. It does not have a CVE identifier. "The vulnerability allows any authenticated user to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on

items.title: Geordie Raises $30 Million for AI Security and Governance Platform
items.description: The funding round was led by Balderton Capital, with additional support from Crosspoint Capital and previous investors General Catalyst and Ten Eleven Ventures. The post Geordie Raises $30 Million for AI Security and Governance Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Agentic AI Isn't Risky; the Way Orgs Deploy It Is
items.description: AI agents aren't black boxes — they're models interacting with software tools. The risk lies in their overlap.

items.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
items.description: Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical, now-patched security flaw impacting FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) deployments to deliver credential-stealing malware. "The campaign abused trusted endpoint management infrastructure to deliver malware across managed endpoints," Arctic Wolf said. "Threat actors disguised the credential stealer payload as a Fortinet endpoint

items.title: Cruise giant Carnival confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
items.description: The company said the threat actor gained access to a limited portion of its IT environment last month after compromising an employee account. By the end of April, Carnival determined that the attacker had copied personal information from its systems.

items.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
items.description: Microsoft has come out strongly in favor of Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD), urging the research community to share their findings and give affected vendors an opportunity to better understand the impact and address them before they are publicly disclosed. The development comes after a researcher named Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) disclosed details of multiple zero-day

items.title: Canadian man gets 33 years for using social media to coerce US children into sending sexual content
items.description: Prosecutors said the man spent years using fake online identities to contact children and manipulate them into sending sexually explicit images and videos.

items.title: Chinese-speaking fraud gang could be stealing millions from 2026 World Cup fans
items.description: Cybercriminals have registered more than 4,300 fraudulent domains impersonating FIFA's official web presence since August 2025.

items.title: Russia conducting daily attacks on UK 'from seabed to cyberspace,' spy chief warns
items.description: Anne Keast-Butler, director of GCHQ, said Russia's actions have prompted the agency to defend subsea cables and energy pipelines in British waters, disrupt Russian networks smuggling sanctioned technology and countering “reckless sabotage and assassination attempts.”

items.title: Focus on Cyber Insurance: How Quantifying Risk Is Reshaping Security
items.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how cyber insurance is forcing organizations to quantify risk, what's covered (and what's not), and why this could be the best thing to happen to cybersecurity.

items.title: BTMOB RAT Spreads Across Brazil, LatAm via MaaS Model
items.description: An advanced remote access Trojan is propagating online. Notably, it's delivered via an operator licensing model and features a no-code malware-development interface.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Reconstructing an Akira Ransomware Kill Chain from Perimeter and Endpoint Logs, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Most Akira write-ups focus on the ransom note or the encryption routine. By the time those show up the interesting forensic work is over. The questions that matter to defenders sit earlier. How did they get in. When did they get domain admin. What did they touch before the binary fired. Those answers live in the days before impact. They sit in two log sources that almost never get joined. The perimeter firewall and the Windows event channel.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 27th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9946, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 26th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9944, (Tue, May 26th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Possible ACR Stealer From Page Impersonating Claude, (Tue, May 26th)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: Microsoft Access VBA, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: Microsoft Access files (Microsoft Office&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Database) can contain VBA code.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-24, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: TeamPCP now operates across three package ecosystems in parallel, it reached GitHub&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s own internal codebase, it trojanized an officially Microsoft-published Python SDK, and it appears to have open-sourced its own framework on GitHub.

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
